name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans up to $500, installment loans up to $1,000, and title loans up to $25,000 at in-store and online locations nationwide.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized, fully accredited short-term lending company that serves millions of customers across the United States. The company has established a large retail footprint with physical branches — including this Jacksonville, FL location in the Commonwealth Village neighborhood — complemented by online application options. With over 200,000 Google reviews and a 4.9 out of 5 rating based on 125,403 customer reviews, the company has built a substantial reputation in the consumer lending space.

At this Jacksonville location, Advance America offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are designed as two-to-four-week short-term loans to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment in multiple payments over a longer term, making them more manageable than payday loans. Title Loans, offered in partnership with LoanCenter, go from $2,000 up to $25,000, using a borrower's paid-off vehicle as collateral. The branch also provides Western Union money transfer services.

What sets this location apart is its bilingual service — staff speak Spanish, which serves Jacksonville's Spanish-speaking community. Customers can apply in person, online, or pre-qualify by phone. Requirements include a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN. Same-day funding is available on loans up to $1,000. Store hours run Monday through Friday with weekend Saturday access, closed Sundays.

Advance America fills a real need for consumers who cannot access bank credit, but it comes with the significant cost typical of the short-term lending industry. Payday loans in particular carry annualized rates that are extremely high by any standard. Title loans carry the added risk of vehicle repossession if the borrower defaults. This is a legitimate option for a genuine financial emergency when all lower-cost alternatives have been exhausted — not a routine cash management tool.
pros: 4.9/5 star rating based on 125,403 verified customer reviews
Same-day funding available on personal loans up to $1,000
Title loans up to $25,000 for consumers needing larger emergency funds
Bilingual (Spanish-speaking) staff available at this location
Multiple channels: apply in-store, online, or pre-qualify by phone
Western Union money transfer services offered alongside loans
Accepts ITIN — accessible to non-SSN holders
cons: Payday loans are 2–4 week terms with high APRs typical of the industry
Title loans require a fully paid-off vehicle as collateral — risk of repossession on default
Title loans are serviced through third-party LoanCenter, not directly by Advance America
Maximum payday loan is only $500, which may be insufficient for larger emergencies
Closed on Sundays, limiting access on weekend emergencies
best_for: Consumers needing $100–$500 fast between paychecks with no bank credit access
Car owners with a paid-off vehicle seeking $2,000–$25,000 in emergency funds
Spanish-speaking borrowers who prefer in-person, bilingual service
Unbanked or thin-file borrowers who need in-store assistance with a loan application
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers facing a short-term cash shortfall who cannot qualify for bank credit and need funds the same day. The main caveat is cost: payday and installment loans carry the high rates standard in this industry, and title loans put a paid-off vehicle at risk — making these products appropriate only as a last resort for genuine emergencies.

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
